samples.xml_description:
Implement an unsigned 8bit multiplier based on pipelining processing. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Input enable signal that controls the multiplication operation. Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 8 bits. Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 8 bits. Output enable signal indicating if the multiplication operation is enabled. Product output signal with a data width of 16 bits. 8 16 A register to store the input enable signal mul_en_in. It has a width of 2 bits. A register to store the input multiplicand mul_a. A register to store the input multiplier mul_b. A register to store the sum of partial products. It has a width of 2N bits. A register to store the output product value. It has a width of 2N bits. A register to store the selected bits from the multiplicand based on the multiplier bits.

Define a input control block to handle input enable signal and store input values.

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin if (!rst_n) begin mul_en_out_reg <= 2'b00; mul_a_reg <= {N{1'b0}}; mul_b_reg <= {N{1'b0}}; end else begin mul_en_out_reg <= {mul_en_out_reg[0], mul_en_in}; if (mul_en_in) begin mul_a_reg <= mul_a; mul_b_reg <= mul_b; end end end

Define partial product generation block to generate partial products based on multiplier bits.

genvar i; generate for (i = 0; i < N; i = i + 1) begin : gen_partial_products assign temp[i] = mul_b_reg[i] ? mul_a_reg : 8'b0; end endgenerate

Define partial sum calculation block to calculate partial sums based on generated partial products.

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin if (!rst_n) begin sum[0] <= 1'b0; for (int i = 1; i < N; i = i + 1) begin sum[i] <= 1'b0; end end else begin sum[0] <= temp[0]; for (int i = 1; i < N; i = i + 1) begin sum[i] <= sum[i-1] + temp[i]; end end end

Define a output control block to handle output enable signal and store output values.

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin if (!rst_n) begin mul_out_reg <= {2*N{1'b0}}; end else begin mul_out_reg <= sum[N-1]; end end

Define the final output assignment based on output enable signal.

assign mul_out = mul_en_out ? mul_out_reg : 16'b0;
